But that amount aims to cover not only climate adaptation, but also mitigation, and loss and damage funding--and is envisioned as a target for wealthy countries to reach in a decade, while poor countries are already (in some cases, literally) underwater. According to a representative who attended Cop30: The needs for adaptation are huge and growing--about $210 billion to $360 billion a year is estimated. You can get private sector money for things that make a profit, like clean energy; the banks will give you money for that because they make the investment back. No private sector company will invest in the things we need for adaptation, like ways to store water from the rains, or helping farmers to grow different crops. Developed countries don't want to give us the money, but they are supposed to. Under the Paris agreement, they agreed in article 9.1 to pay us. We know that we did not create this crisis--they did--but we are the ones who are suffering. Ignoring the death trap of fossil fuel dependence Over 80 countries advocated at Cop30 for an agreement that would help support a , but they were blocked by an equal number of others: the U.S., which refused to even attend; China, which attended but declined to take a leadership role given the absence of the U.S.; and petrostates including Saudi Arabia and Russia, which economically depend on fossil fuel exports and have widely been spared by natural disasters fueled by the climate crisis. As a result, the agreements emerging from Cop30 omitted any reference to the fossil fuels driving climate chaos. A member of the European parliament's delegation to Cop30 named Bas Eickhout the dynamic in simple terms: [N]o deal is better than a bad deal. Failure to reach an agreement on a roadmap to transition away from fossil fuels would not only be a big win for petrostates, but also for Trump and his hard-right allies. No one can seriously expect us to win the fight on the climate crisis if we don't deal with the elephant in the room: phasing out fossil fuels. Latin American countries including Colombia, Panama and Uruguay also objected to the process, which Colombia's negotiator characterized as "[a] consensus imposed under climate denialism...a ." Colombia joined the Netherlands in announcing plans to host a separate conference next year focused on the transition away from fossil fuels. Maina Talia, the environment minister of Tuvalu (an island nation in the South Pacific) , "We have taken this step because, simply, we cannot wait any longer. The Pacific came to Cop30 demanding a survival roadmap away from fossil fuels. Yet this text does not even name the threat to our existence. This process is failing us so we will not wait." A saving grace? During the COVID-19 pandemic, global trade briefly ground to a halt, leading to a in greenhouse gas emissions. For instance, one study estimated that, in April 2020, daily carbon dioxide emissions declined 17% from the preceding baseline. This relationship between economic recession and reductions in greenhouse gas emissions is consistent. Another study that greenhouse gas emissions fell by 1.3% during the global depression following the 2008 financial crisis, but then increased by nearly 6% in 2010, bringing CO2 emission rates back to their preceding level. These studies suggest that the most meaningful acts promoting climate justice are those that undermine industrial economies, erode capitalism, and build sustainable alternatives. As long as I'm on that subject, I urge every American to participate in a this holiday season and beyond. Meanwhile, as it happens, nothing in the past half century has done as much to devastate the global economy as the policies of the Trump administration. As part of an ongoing series, I've this year exploring Trump's impact on the American economy. From ham-fisted tariffs to labor shortages driven by lawless immigration enforcement, Trump has done more to stop greenhouse gasses--by undermining the American economy--than any president in history. Abundant ironies Of course, the president is a relentless capitalist. His focus on wealth is so extreme that it appears to frequently eclipse his presidency, or alternatively it. But while the figure of the president might be no less committed to capitalism than who have him for years, his policy legacy appears to be at odds with his personal aims. Despite being a wealthy president who identifies primarily as a capitalist, Trump has done as much to diminish greenhouse emissions as any political figure in the world. It is certainly ironic that someone widely described--even a fascist would prove so inadvertently helpful in the global struggle for climate justice. But, given the scale of his (even unwitting) contribution, few figures seem to have done as much to deserve the Nobel prize committee's recognition. You're currently a free subscriber to . For the full experience, | | | | | | | (C) 2025 Shahid Buttar South Lake Tahoe, CA 96150 ? ?

Peter,
🚨 Marjorie Taylor Greene just announced her resignation from Congress effective January 5, 2026.
There will be a special election to replace her.
My name is Shawn Harris. I'm a Democrat, a husband, a father, a Retired Brigadier General, a farmer, and I intend to win that election for Georgia and for the American people.
[If you are in a position to support my campaign, I need you now more than ever. What if I told you that I’m the Democrat and retired U.S. Navy Rear Admiral running to defeat Lauren Boebert? What if I told you that Adam Frisch only lost to Lauren Boebert by 546 votes in the closest race in the country? What if I told you that Lauren Boebert is as unpopular as ever among voters? What if I told you that winning this seat would guarantee that we take back the House for Democrats in 2026? And what if I told you that we can finally send Lauren Boebert packing – but only if grassroots donors like you chip in now to my campaign? We need to raise another $7,500 before midnight tonight if we’re going to defeat Lauren Boebert and flip this seat blue, but right now, we’re still very short. The only way I’ll be able to fight back against the GOP Super PACs and win is if you give right now: [Please, will you split a donation right now between my campaign and Blue Amp Action – every dollar you can afford before midnight – to help me defeat Lauren Boebert, flip this seat blue, and take back the House for Democrats?](https://default.salsalabs.org/T7d513c30-e184-4c19-a2f2-9bcd1f1f7db1/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) I’ll never be able to thank you enough for being on this team and joining me on this journey. We at Blue Amp Action are dedicated to liberal, open societies, where reason and science are applied to solve our problems; where civil rights--including voting rights!--are protected for all. We fight for these values every day. Speaking up on our platforms, raising the voices and the political prospects of liberal pols and pundits. **And savvy grassroots activists like you know, just like we do, that we can’t stop watching the people we help elect:_ after the elections, we have to keep organizing support for the policies we like and pushing back against the ones we don’t._** **__When the public tunes out between elections and the only people our elected officials hear from are the paid lobbyists of wealthy corporations, our government stops being accountable to the needs of ordinary families.__** **Democracy never sleeps, and neither can we.** So we ask that you support our work, so we can continue to engage in this fight every moment of every day. So our kids grow up in a democracy, not a dystopia. Thank you for your support for all we do! — _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tfc169e68-bb66-47d2-8b32-522e37a992e7/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._

subject: Castle Auction, Tyson Foods Layoff, and a Thanksgiving Travel Record
body:
A 19,359-square-foot Fort Worth mansion with castle-like turrets and stone interiors is [hitting the auction block](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGu4yAQRb8GdxPB2JC4oEjj7q30ttnSwmYcI2GIYBLHf79Kdv_g1aM5997z52v8ciGGdPvlNrL8WvKYaK-RmKmMs6scyT1mDjnxUXNacvY1uhElalAKsBu9C_EYu8bbSfpeNWTV2bT9RRutm9WiJINnLY2Wxvhp0r7TiNNFL2j63s9NsG-YUtgplFK3p36ZWul15wkn186L6CT5wLmceCWml6tLPPKTymnOWxPtynyvor0KHAQO-76ftpreN4EDJXhUgcOWEx0Ch0IuUmXHJHBwsOTCsOfCK_zbCnlP5GE6wMEnCopLNyoV6EUzhAqP-_sL_kuBSgQh1eA_wALXq_r-rpffTbH-vocYQzVn0cnb5kL8FOafaX5a_BsAAP__f02UJQ) next month after being listed for $11.75 million.

# Monday, November 24, 2025
---|---
**Good morning!** On this day in 1835, lawmakers for the provisional government of Texas established the [Texas Rangers](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0DFuwyAUgOHT4C2WeRgSDwxdvKVrR-sZHjYqgQhe4-T2VXOFXuD7pf_rulwxppi3T7yR5WcoS6ajJWKmujhsnAh_HMeS-dVKDqX4lnCBAfRJyhOMi8eYXsvYebsOfpIdWXk2arpoo3W3W-V8cIBGOTeZ4CCgV3QZAaQzZC66i_YPkxJGCcOgVT-FVQ1ej55gReWCGAfykUvteSemJ7aQXuVBtXfl1iW7M9-bUB8CZgHzcRw9tx1LTjFTX-omYN4x-7WUbwEzZa6RmoD5TZ0q5o1q66r19yOmFJs5i3HYbhjTO8D_2_Kw8BsAAP__NYl5uQ). The fighting force achieved worldwide fame during the Mexican War, earning the moniker "los diablos Tejanos" for their success in repelling Mexican guerrillas. Today, the Texas Rangers are an elite crime-fighting unit within the Department of Public Safety. Arch Manning is the first Texas Longhorn to score passing, rushing, and receiving touchdowns [in the same game](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkD2O3SAYAE-DOyzAYJ4LijR0mzalxc9ng4ThCb5dr28fba6QeqTRaP587B8ul1zP3-4Cg99H2yvcowAi9D24gQXcZ8DcKj6j1aO1OIrbBROKck6F3KPL5dnlFI1nceMTGK7XZXupVakpmSWsSq-BMwk6KK9eXr6Y1hKi1MxrP2XzI-NcSC4YU8u8HX5hUckIwrslHEQyiBlbnzEBwrcbR3naF_Q5tGsqJiG-B1l-EWGJsPd9zzDe9QcSYUMrBU6gR2voXSlE2IGtP0TYnQibIxFWaraum1ZEWNdDoperNdeTHrkPpKXVM7VeaXAYEsXU203750gU49RNfN-5lDxWTSQ7L5fLvyz8v5lfRvwNAAD__3CQii0). He accounted for six scores and threw for 389 yards in Saturday's 52-37 win over Arkansas. ******Keep an eye on your inbox.** We’re about to send an important message from our sponsor, [GoldCo](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkT2vnTAMhn9N2HIU54vDwFCVst0rdarUBYXEQKRAjog53PPvK_oxVZ3q0bKfx9b77W14czHFbX53K7b0MeVhw7MkJMJ98K5QQnd4inmjV8nblHMoyQ1SSMMBuNRDcDG9Bl2FdhShgQpbqK1q7sYaUy2tAwAtbW3B3vU0BjspbQRoVFBPTYAqthcMQGqQQhh1a6ZRiWB0QDk65SemBYZIeb_RgoQfrkzplZ-433xeq9QuRI_C1Ccmeyb78zxvjz0TeorbvL6Ke8ZtLhxXF9O1wWTvVtyjd1vhY5xnLMRp2dERBxBGmguker8x1d2N4bYxXDdSMGlTYaoTAviXi8b7X3fwDkP0jjAwacvkY2CqqwV8XcRVeLx__8ykPWgdvFsfLs7bPwdWDPFYmep-Gn43Sz52j0x1fwmrvQ2PM6YUi62ZFvOfLyv6vzSfrfwRAAD__3kysao), about the financial risks exposed by the recent government shutdown and what Americans can do to protect their retirement savings. If stubborn nail fungus has refused to go away, this surprising natural “purple soak” method from today's sponsor, [Pronail Complex](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLuOpDAQAL_GZIv8Ag-Bg5VWZDvphaiN24OlBiPcwzB_f5r7hctLVVL9-Z1-IVPeHndY0fOVyrThqxIy4zHNUJkQnjPnsvG7li2VEivBpKXuvpT60naKkOk92Sb6IOOgGvTK9Wa4dX3XNYsPABh6sBiScyY5kHGIMt2CC046e2uy_8iU0lZpKTvTDikYGTsbUQcwcxJWYsxcjpYXZLygJnqXE492LmtDfmHeqzDfQo9Cj_tRNsg0l3UnvD6I0OOZI5Z2X3ZhRkgpUwZGYX5CoQg7XkronnMU5ifR-qwcMWp7L6fuvpvDx_2ViXLtnbDysUKmf2X-v1-n138DAAD__zmqgag), may be the simple, fast-acting solution you’ve been hoping for.

## Officer Shot in Road Rage Incident
An off-duty Houston police officer was injured in a [road rage shooting](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkM_q2zAQhJ9Gvm2Q5H_xwYdcfPuVFgqFXsxGWsVLZSlImzh5-5K-Qm8DA8N836-v9Qs5crp9w51meYW8JjpqJBEqq8MqkfDhhHOSd80p5OxrxNVq24MxYLvVI8f32jV-vmo_mYZmMw7tdO6Hvm-2eXChbzutz9PYkvfoJ-_a0ZppmMJounPD82fMGNsZq3XfnqZwbbXvO0_2iq0LqtPkWXI5yUZCL6whvvOTysnlvYnzJnKvqr0ouyi7HMdx2mv6dMoulOBRlV0-UMourvBOyi5bflTJCbAUqgKyFSKoRH_gyZ4yYBAqkEMA_5D3J7CjAlyhblmAE-D9joWSQMnooeCNgJNjT0mUXbDA5WJ-_P5efzZl9veDY-Q6jKrTtx05_nsu_-f7Odu_AQAA__9zy5g3) as he was driving to work on Saturday night. According to a preliminary police report, a young male driver "making a bad decision" got out of his vehicle at an intersection and fired into the officer's vehicle, striking his shoulder. Jake Parker, a three-year veteran of the department, [returned fire](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGO3CAYhJ8Gd6zgx6zjgiIN3aVNaWH4sVFYsOD3-fbtI0d5g-umGM03M78_lg-XcirbL_dCQ1-xLgWvnpEI2-Jdp4zu9JRqoXevJdYaenYLCNBcSg7jElzK72UcgllFmOWARk5PNf_QT62H3cyTnp-IGgAkTgFWpdZZTKMA0OsapyGZO0xKGCUIodVjjqsSQY8BYXXKRzYKDIlqe9COhF-ux_yun9gevr6GbHaiozP1k4FlYK_revic_B_Y69mpltvFwN6zGNhcvcsM7M1kYKW8tWJg9yPwGmPy2HjfK_FwtlQ23s9-oCcMvFUXeHMb8lR8CliIp8JLbbRf2In_5zGwQzPhuFLOqT8nNort5VL-15a-9_Gngb8BAAD__9xHkkY) and drove himself to a nearby police station, where he was transported to a hospital in stable condition. Police tracked the shooter's vehicle using license plate readers and an air unit. A man and two women [were arrested](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkDGO5CAURE-DM1rwARsHBJs4m003tL7h00aLTQuY8fTtV94rTFgqqVTv_flYPzDldD5_40Guf8eynnS1TL1TXT22ngk_fU_l7O9WzlhKaBlXEGC4lBz0GjDl96qH4DYRZjmQk9OoZmtGY4bdGe8jjUIDggwoN6RJYaCgxCxFMDgkd49JCVqCEEY95rgpEYwOBBsqH5kWFFIv9dF36vSNLeZ3-aL68OUYstt7fzWmfjFYGCzXdT3-7uXzLhksWHvymRgsNxeDxdd03LHEmDxV3vbS-U7Hq3XCwJ_U2kmVwQLWcIzCGjNKLqUxXJMGPs_WclCTn5S1FOw8VBdeV8o5tXFiWjwPTPn_tf4zoV8O_gUAAP__YlWIvQ) without further incident, and police recovered a gun linked to the shooting. Mayor John Whitmire visited Parker at the hospital, where many of his colleagues had gathered in a show of support. Whitmire [condemned](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkDFvgzAUhH8NbEb2syHN4KELW7p2RMZ-DpYcHrJfQvj3FVX_QdfT3enu-75NN5dyWu9f7oGW35GmFfeakRnL5F3ljO7pOdHKR6U1EoWa3QQSeqGUADMFl_IxmTbYWYaratGqy6CvH_3Q9-1ipQYPRocg_TwobyJehl4PziCADz62yZ5lSoFRIGWvu2uctQy9CQiz0z42RmJITKXjBRnfrsZ80AtL5-nRZrswb7XRnw2MDYz7vneR3jAs9KxM62lqYDxfNTD-iWKjnDwKijF5LKIuxKKQC6K4O4q0-hRwZaFFfdYNPVfhz2A42mLDtqecUx0ujZH3h0v5dwj_D9_Lwk8AAAD__0vkiKA) generalized criticism of law enforcement, suggesting such rhetoric could encourage "bad actors."
## Video of Woman in Labor Sparks Outrage
The family of a woman forced to fill out paperwork while in active labor at a Mesquite hospital has [hired a well-known civil rights law firm](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL1uhDAQhJ8GOiPbmL_CRRq6S5sSLXgBKwtG9h7cvX3EvUK6KUaj-b6fx_AAT35fvmFDy685DDteiZAZ4zBBYkJ4TuzDzu8U9jkElwgGLXUllBLaDA48vQeTOztK16kcrWrqsmuruqry1bZt29VmbAFgVNKUTaNbWU-tkzAZbEzu7T2mlDZKS1mVRTePpXSVcahHKKc5MxKd5xALXpHxBWmmdzgxFlPYcrIr85Gy8ivTfab767qKObzMjXEXMt3fMdP96SOQOL3DILawCYIxROGACJJYQzo8A4l0QPxNIjw5woKCcAES8DGQR-uOyxP5VDeZkcsGnj4n-H_qTqv_AgAA__9pdoZu) to represent them in a lawsuit. A video of the encounter at Dallas Regional Medical Center has garnered millions of views on social media and prompted [responses from local lawmakers,](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkD2O2zAQhU8jdWOIFGlHBQs36jZBiiClMBaH8gBD0iC51ur2gfYK6R7wgPfz_f1YPpCF0_YTI7n2FfKSaK9CrVFZVqxNCD_Xxjm1o-YUcvZVcNGDtqAUaLN4ZDkW03v3GPykenLqdh2nH_Zqbf90JpiAo1VXixiC9VNAq7UNxit9s9PaszvDlNJG6WGw42UKj3Hw1njSDxzX0JmBPLdcLu1Jjb6wBjnym8plzbEX92ztVbvx3um50_O-75dY0-l1eqYEn7XT83mq0_O3DhhZDsgBXoW2hKlBzBE4wZsLCngUwQqFNs4JBSJ5XlFgpdSowJs9ZXhyoQpCGwo0wrMLC9zv6vef49e9L86_dhbher11ZtgisnzPbf8H-e30vwAAAP__TEqUPg) who have discussed the "deeply concerning" incident with hospital staff. The woman reportedly waited [more than 30 minutes](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkDuu4yAUhleDOyzAYMcFxTTuMu2U1jGP-EjHEBnGJLu_SrZw61__6_t3X--AhOnxF45g6yvmNYVWKNQaztVBqRTgv6uYU32XnGLOvhCsSijDpeRKrx6Q3qvuvN2En2UXrJzGYb6Z0Zhut6ORk5g2A4Pz0zTfPMh53qKLY1BmFKZD-wmTUmmphDBDP8dtEN5oH9QGg4tMi-Cx5rOve6jhBSXSO1_h7F0-OrJ7rc_Chj9MLUwtrbU-bc7H9pGZWj5_mFooOyCmlgtPIH6hD5m3fEDimDjBlk_eACumB_dABIV_q_ieyxPr1zrMetSTYmrpTuufDYmwjBPT4nEA0ndO_R3Ey6qfAAAA___l24fA) at the hospital before she was taken to labor and delivery, where her son was born minutes later. The video shows her screaming through contractions as a nurse asks about her medical history. "_It was paperwork over life_ ," [said the woman’s mother](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0L2O3SAQxfGnwd1cwRj8UbhI427TprQGGNZI2KyAtddvH928QuojHen3__OxfVBM8fz8TQcv7Sfk7eS7Jm6Ny-aotsT07VrMZ3tqPkPOvibaUKIBpQD15immZ9OdX6z0s-p4UePQz5MZjOn2BdEOo-_Z9tLpiYPT0zQoZWY9a5aj6eLyPlMKtUIpTf-ag-2lN9ozWupdEFqyjy2XV9u58Q_VkJ58cXm5fHRp2Vv7qqL_JXAVuN73_boD0XsUuFJp0SUWuL5dAtedKbVd4BroiOkBcu67coU916_YKEEO4DnRwx4cFYY7H3QCuRYvhkQ2F6DQuMAVCyW4oucscMVphF67UapZAhnpQeMcwNqggL1GLefeW-O6svivO6YU6zAKLT8PiumfpP1f_2vBvwEAAP__MVaZ_Q), who reported that the nurse told her, "_The quicker you sign, the quicker we can get her in the back_." The hospital is investigating the incident, and the nurse in the video has been placed on leave.
## Fort Worth Castle Going to Auction
A 19,359-square-foot Fort Worth mansion with castle-like turrets and stone interiors is [hitting the auction block](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGu4yAQRb8GdxPB2JC4oEjj7q30ttnSwmYcI2GIYBLHf79Kdv_g1aM5997z52v8ciGGdPvlNrL8WvKYaK-RmKmMs6scyT1mDjnxUXNacvY1uhElalAKsBu9C_EYu8bbSfpeNWTV2bT9RRutm9WiJINnLY2Wxvhp0r7TiNNFL2j63s9NsG-YUtgplFK3p36ZWul15wkn186L6CT5wLmceCWml6tLPPKTymnOWxPtynyvor0KHAQO-76ftpreN4EDJXhUgcOWEx0Ch0IuUmXHJHBwsOTCsOfCK_zbCnlP5GE6wMEnCopLNyoV6EUzhAqP-_sL_kuBSgQh1eA_wALXq_r-rpffTbH-vocYQzVn0cnb5kL8FOafaX5a_BsAAP__f02UJQ) next month after being listed for $11.75 million. The home was built in 2006 for XTO founder and Texas Rangers co-owner Bob Simpson and his wife, who [sold the property](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkDvOpDAQhE9jMpDd5hk42IRsNt0QNbg9WGrwyO553X7F7hH-sKRSlb7vz225YeR43n_jQU4-IS0nvQuTCOVlwyJM-NwkplO-JZ0hJV8YF9DQ1cbU0C4eI3-XtvJu1X4yFTkz9HYau77rqt2NftR-HdCQ6cfBw7BhILDW0NqC1aaK7hozBloDWne2mcJqte9aT7Ci3YJqNfkoKTeyk9AHS-BvelFutnRU7HaRR1H2l4JZwRxSlnfKsjfbk-WZ6cDHVVQwX2QK5kzINRVBoSvheadc6v-s9TQMtl6JGWuhnFHBXGXnH-_IHEs_qFbfD4z871t-Zuzl4G8AAAD__5Zbge4) in 2021. It features seven bedrooms, 13 full and five half bathrooms, gourmet kitchens, a climate-controlled wine cellar, and living spaces with vaulted ceilings and ornate finishes. The 2.12-acre property offers [indoor and outdoor pools](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGO3CAURb8Gd4zgAeOhcJHG3aZNaT3gMUbCZgVk2fn7yKP8wdZXOrrn_PnYPjDldD5_40FL_45lO2m0TL1T3Ty2ngn_-p7K2V-tnLGU0DJuIMBwKTnoLWDKr01PYXEiWDnRIue7sg9zN2bal2iDQqmcNjYaMPOsTVSzIu8cOO3klJYLJiVoCUIYdbPRKRGMDgQOlY9MCwqpl3rrO3X6xhbzq3xRvflyTHnZe_9sTP1isDJYxxi3ERGvkcGKtSeficF6eTFYc_GYGaxvEK94Pqk2XsZJlR94tlROvpeDeKN8heH_9XkstfNRat8ZrPCYuQ7euxCAW68010IIbu_Rcim1f9gYQAea6hI-R8o5tfvMtHgemPL7d_9Z7a8F_gUAAP__M6GUdg), a private pond, and a tennis court. Bids for the Dec. 3 auction are expected to start between $4 million and $6.5 million, according to Concierge Auctions. The mansion is one of the most recognizable landmarks in the prestigious Montserrat neighborhood, which includes several other castle-like estates and was once home to pop star Selena Gomez.

According to a preliminary police report, a young male driver "making a bad decision" got out of his vehicle at an intersection and fired into the officer's vehicle, striking his shoulder. Jake Parker, a three-year veteran of the department, and drove himself to a nearby police station, where he was transported to a hospital in stable condition. Police tracked the shooter's vehicle using license plate readers and an air unit. A man and two women without further incident, and police recovered a gun linked to the shooting. Mayor John Whitmire visited Parker at the hospital, where many of his colleagues had gathered in a show of support. Whitmire generalized criticism of law enforcement, suggesting such rhetoric could encourage "bad actors." Video of Woman in Labor Sparks Outrage The family of a woman forced to fill out paperwork while in active labor at a Mesquite hospital has to represent them in a lawsuit. A video of the encounter at Dallas Regional Medical Center has garnered millions of views on social media and prompted who have discussed the "deeply concerning" incident with hospital staff. The woman reportedly waited at the hospital before she was taken to labor and delivery, where her son was born minutes later. The video shows her screaming through contractions as a nurse asks about her medical history. "It was paperwork over life ," , who reported that the nurse told her, "The quicker you sign, the quicker we can get her in the back." The hospital is investigating the incident, and the nurse in the video has been placed on leave. Fort Worth Castle Going to Auction A 19,359-square-foot Fort Worth mansion with castle-like turrets and stone interiors is next month after being listed for $11.75 million. The home was built in 2006 for XTO founder and Texas Rangers co-owner Bob Simpson and his wife, who in 2021. It features seven bedrooms, 13 full and five half bathrooms, gourmet kitchens, a climate-controlled wine cellar, and living spaces with vaulted ceilings and ornate finishes. The 2.12-acre property offers , a private pond, and a tennis court. Bids for the Dec. 3 auction are expected to start between $4 million and $6.5 million, according to Concierge Auctions. The mansion is one of the most recognizable landmarks in the prestigious Montserrat neighborhood, which includes several other castle-like estates and was once home to pop star Selena Gomez. | Flying together with our sponsor What if there was a natural way to get rid of nail fungus... name: DNC Community Engagement
subject: Bilingual (ENG/SPN) Know Your Rights to Vote Informational Session – Dec 4, at 6 PM ET
body:
| | | | | [|
---](https://collegedems.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b3a52587106e5f265f227782b&id=a777b4db16&e=d6c5c7d7ec)
|
---
| |
# “Know Your Rights to Vote” Informational Session
---
| |

The right to vote is a fundamental pillar of our democracy. Across the country, communities are coming together to protect this right and ensure every voter’s voice is heard. But we cannot do this work alone.
We invite you to join the **Democratic National Committee** and **Latino Victory Fund** for the informational session **“Know Your Rights to Vote”** on **Thursday, December 4, at 6 p.m. ET.**
During this session, you’ll learn about your rights at the polls, the legal protections available, and the actions we’re taking to ensure free, fair, and accessible elections. We have critical runoff elections this December, from Nashville to Miami.
---
| [REGISTER HERE](https://collegedems.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b3a52587106e5f265f227782b&id=407670d160&e=d6c5c7d7ec)
---
|
---
| |
# Sesión Informativa “Conozca Sus Derechos al Votar”
---
| |
El derecho al voto es un pilar fundamental de nuestra democracia. En todo el país, comunidades enteras se están organizando para defender este derecho y proteger la voz de cada votante. Pero este trabajo no lo podemos hacer solos.
Por eso, le invitamos a unirse al **Comité Nacional Demócrata** y a **Latino Victory Fund** para una sesión informativa bilingüe sobre **“Conozca Sus Derechos al Votar”** el **jueves 4 de diciembre a las 6 PM ET**.
Durante esta sesión aprenderá sobre sus derechos en las cajas de votación, las protecciones legales disponibles y las acciones que estamos tomando para garantizar elecciones libres, justas y accesibles. Tenemos elecciones de segunda vuelta decisivas este diciembre, desde Nashville hasta Miami.
---
| [REGÍSTRESE AQUÍ](https://collegedems.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b3a52587106e5f265f227782b&id=d42a96d430&e=d6c5c7d7ec)

name: Lawrence Reed
subject: A troubling milestone for America
body:
Gallup recently released data showing a worrisome trend: a growing positive perception of socialism.

|
---
| | [](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zpk8l1mi3chbcm76cqc4m7tu08hcevdfev1qsqb0_rp22sh2s8i6eor1c1g66pj6c3h2bs) Friend,

Gallup recently released data showing a worrisome trend: a growing positive perception of socialism.

This news shows us that many people today have a profound misunderstanding of history and human nature.

When people think that socialism means “fairness” or “sharing,” they forget what it really entails: **state control over individual choice, innovation, and opportunity.**

The Foundation for Economic Education (FEE) has been warning about the dangers and failures of socialism for nearly 80 years. **Our mission is to inspire, educate, and connect future leaders with the economic, ethical, and legal principles of a free society.**

If you’ve never donated to FEE, or if it’s been over a year since your last gift, a member of FEE’s Board will DOUBLE — or even TRIPLE — your donation!
[MATCH MY GIFT](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zc3gt3vn5rdneo62mecfvjg80o2vqir8hiijn1c0_rp22sh2s8i6eor1c1g66pj6c3h2bs)
[To help share these truths, I’d like to send you a digital copy of _Reflections on the Failure of Socialism_ — a powerful book we use to show young people what history has already proven about socialism’s empty promises — when you donate today.](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zmhsbb0kc54dhie08sja65kahqts8makdbgqnpb0_rp22sh2s8i6eor1c1g66pj6c3h2bs)
[](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zd3q0ol4f55vtnp40unltjkeqfooh94cf2gqk4t8_rp22sh2s8i6eor1c1g66pj6c3h2bs)
As Max Eastman warns in his book: _“Yearning to do good and obsessed by the power of the state to do it... they are destroying in the name of social welfare the foundations of freedom.”_

At FEE, we know that liberty must be chosen, learned, and lived anew in every generation. This is why sparking curiosity, deepening understanding, and building future advocates for liberty is at the heart of everything we do.

**Through our classroom programs, teacher training, online content, and transformative in-person events, we teach young people the truth!**

We’re showing them that:
* Innovation and voluntary exchange create prosperity for everyone.
* Central planning and government control lead to poverty, stagnation, and oppression.
* Freedom is both a moral and practical necessity for human dignity and opportunity.

One of the tools we use to teach these lessons is _**Reflections on the Failure of Socialism**_. This powerful book shows students, with history and logic, why socialism fails — again and again — and why freedom works. [And I will send you a FREE digital copy of the book when you donate today. As a bonus, your first-time gift will be MATCHED!](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zu2muhm664q7d8081uv21forhlqie5vrpbi54hm8_rp22sh2s8i6eor1c1g66pj6c3h2bs)
[Donate $35 → $70](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z871cmbdakk7j7pj3gipveqondvpv7nc79dp3csg_rp22sh2s8i6eor1c1g66pj6c3h2bs)

[Donate $50 → $100](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zh6jmu3c2f260df30bd2ibjo1tsk336e9bd1p83o_rp22sh2s8i6eor1c1g66pj6c3h2bs)

[Donate $100 → $200](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zr301kjt12quvgpmv9fle8vmtt1malf7l2ihofro_rp22sh2s8i6eor1c1g66pj6c3h2bs)

[Donate $500 → $1,000](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zrfte8gn3bv5hhbd4cu19cig0jl2vlr1s90qugn8_rp22sh2s8i6eor1c1g66pj6c3h2bs)

[Donate $1,0000 → $3,000](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zlucofsid7jtgih1ovqgr5n7kko9nn5a80ii53q8_rp22sh2s8i6eor1c1g66pj6c3h2bs)

[Donate $1,500 → $4,500](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zr19f4k4l6c7ppkheu22pbca7f1jnabu3u8v4f98_rp22sh2s8i6eor1c1g66pj6c3h2bs)

[Donate Any Amount](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zu99paugtjsg0skblpul5gmnva86h2caurqd165o_rp22sh2s8i6eor1c1g66pj6c3h2bs)
Back in the 1980s, long before I became President Emeritus of FEE, I was a young American in Poland, determined to bring the ideas of liberty to those trapped behind the Iron Curtain.

I didn’t just see the failures of socialism firsthand — I lived among them.

I remember seeing fear in people’s eyes — fear of speaking their minds, fear of their own government.

There were empty shelves and full prisons.

**I saw that under socialism, everyone was equal... equally poor, equally powerless, equally oppressed.**

When I finally came home, I made a promise: I would spend the rest of my life teaching others what socialism really means — and why freedom is the only system that allows humanity to flourish.

Because if we don’t equip young people with these truths, they risk repeating these mistakes.

[You can help FEE show young people what history has already proven about socialism’s empty promises by donating today.](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zq6antf0ka3fiqb7p1ialv7htn9mnj36pgjmsacg_rp22sh2s8i6eor1c1g66pj6c3h2bs)
[Donate](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z41of3scb0rqgcsioa6slql1mf9o5uh1vl5i4jio_rp22sh2s8i6eor1c1g66pj6c3h2bs)
In liberty,

Lawrence W. Reed
President Emeritus, Humphreys Family Senior Fellow, and Ron Manners Global Ambassador for Liberty

**P.S.** **LIMITED-TIME MATCH!** If you’ve never donated to FEE — or if it’s been a year since your last gift — a member of FEE’s Board will **MATCH** your donation. Gifts under $1,000 will be **DOUBLED** , and gifts between $1,000-$5,000 will be **TRIPLED**.
[Get Your Donation MATCHED](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zfro7mn315f2mej9dn8vp6lcme5lu9udiid01hl8_rp22sh2s8i6eor1c1g66pj6c3h2bs)

_FEE is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit educational organization, so donations made to FEE are tax-deductible to the full extent allowable by law._

body:
Well, Trump's hold on the Republican Party loosened a lot last week, as hundreds of congressmen voted to release the Epstein Files over his objections.

---|---|---

---|---|---
* * *
# [Trump on the Wane](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=287304&post_id=179765911&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc5NzY1OTExLCJpYXQiOjE3NjM5ODk2MTcsImV4cCI6MTc2NjU4MTYxNywiaXNzIjoicHViLTI4NzMwNCIsInN1YiI6InBvc3QtcmVhY3Rpb24ifQ.6gdxHh2rlBaqJNv7aPmvHxL8xxcRmBdmY0enzVAzHOs)
| | [Andrew Yang](https://substack.com/@andrewyang)
---
| Nov 24
---
| [](https://substack.com/@andrewyang)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=287304&post_id=179765911&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc5NzY1OTExLCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc2Mzk4OTYxNywiZXhwIjoxNzY2NTgxNjE3LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMjg3MzA0Iiwic3ViIjoicmVhY3Rpb24ifQ.M5630eTvsyjKSFhwSRfuwtbDBwecLKW5AELVZ3C42WU&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=287304&post_id=179765911&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc5NzY1OTExLCJpYXQiOjE3NjM5ODk2MTcsImV4cCI6MTc2NjU4MTYxNywiaXNzIjoicHViLTI4NzMwNCIsInN1YiI6InBvc3QtcmVhY3Rpb24ifQ.6gdxHh2rlBaqJNv7aPmvHxL8xxcRmBdmY0enzVAzHOs&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=287304&post_id=179765911&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc5NzY1OTExLCJpYXQiOjE3NjM5ODk2MTcsImV4cCI6MTc2NjU4MTYxNywiaXNzIjoicHViLTI4NzMwNCIsInN1YiI6InBvc3QtcmVhY3Rpb24ifQ.6gdxHh2rlBaqJNv7aPmvHxL8xxcRmBdmY0enzVAzHOs)
---
| | [](https://substack..IJNz78qR5rmDseptxHafyz6p2TqX3ivaXZIWxvSbvcw?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/andrewyang/p/trump-on-the-wane?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

Well, Trump's hold on the Republican Party loosened a lot last week, as hundreds of congressmen voted to release the Epstein Files over his objections.
It wasn't just that vote. The November elections were a bad look for Republicans, signaling that the midterms may harbor bad news for the party in power. Voters aren't seeing any progress on inflation. The Supreme Court seems skeptical that Trump's tariff powers are legally appropriate as tariffs are normally under Congressional control. His redistricting campaign is hitting stumbling blocks in Texas and Indiana. Infighting has arisen within the administration.
Also Trump himself seems . . . physically and mentally weaker. It feels like he's fading out and becoming more lame duck than dictator before our eyes. Trump's positive meeting with Zohran could be interpreted in this light - it felt like an aging uncle commending the next generation.
I confess to being surprised by this. I thought that Trump's maximalist bullying approach would hold sway over his Party until at least the midterms. He's falling prey to the most basic of problems, which is that he can't actually make anything better in terms of groceries and prices, and the people are turning on him. He's 79 in an office that accelerates aging. It doesn't help that he seems preoccupied with ballrooms and gold decor while the frustration deepens.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/b4cb8421-26ed-4181-9ba9-7571332dcfba?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Kara Swisher said last week on the Pivot Tour, "I don't think Trump finishes the term." I think she may be on to something.
What does this mean? Democrats will cheer that this is their chance, as in the zero-sum version of American politics, Dems feel better and better about their 2026 prospects. Most of the major Democrats are jockeying for position in the presidential primary. I spoke to South Carolina Democrats recently and they are already vetting candidates. Not much will be resolved on that side until the Democrats hash out their own leadership contest in about 28 months. That's a long time from now.
The heir apparent to MAGA is J.D. Vance; he also naturally ascends as VP if Trump steps down or is indisposed for any reason. Vance and Trump's children seem to have formed a deep relationship.
I'm dubious though that Vance would be able to keep MAGA together. Trump has unique appeal and his own celebrity and gravitas. He's a performer and movement leader. Those traits aren't really transferable. I think the party fragments. Marjorie Taylor Greene stepping down doesn't necessarily mean that the internal strife will end.
Still, if I'm sitting here trying to figure out what comes next, so are a lot of other people. The figure who has dominated American politics for much of the last decade may be passing from view. Will Trump continue to defy gravity? This time feels different. In my experience, things often end slowly, slowly, slowly . . . and then all at once.
To build the next chapter in American politics, check out [Forward](https://substack.com/redirect/84df3048-4edc-44f5-acfc-c41f613c05b4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) - we have some very exciting announcements coming. Next up on the [Offline](https://substack.com/redirect/85198315-4225-4261-988e-4fc9a1991668?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) Tour are Boston, Baltimore, and back to NYC. Email matt@noblemobile.com if you want a few months off from your wireless bill and want to be rewarded with [Noble Mobile](https://substack.com/redirect/7086df4a-7252-4d1f-a869-799548086268?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). clean_body: Well, Trump's hold on the Republican Party loosened a lot last week, as hundreds of congressmen voted to release the Epstein Files over his objections. | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more | | ---|---|--- * | | | Nov 24 | | | | | | | | | | | | Well, Trump's hold on the Republican Party loosened a lot last week, as hundreds of congressmen voted to release the Epstein Files over his objections. It wasn't just that vote. The November elections were a bad look for Republicans, signaling that the midterms may harbor bad news for the party in power. Voters aren't seeing any progress on inflation. The Supreme Court seems skeptical that Trump's tariff powers are legally appropriate as tariffs are normally under Congressional control. His redistricting campaign is hitting stumbling blocks in Texas and Indiana. Infighting has arisen within the administration. Also Trump himself seems . . . physically and mentally weaker. It feels like he's fading out and becoming more lame duck than dictator before our eyes. Trump's positive meeting with Zohran could be interpreted in this light - it felt like an aging uncle commending the next generation. I confess to being surprised by this. I thought that Trump's maximalist bullying approach would hold sway over his Party until at least the midterms. He's falling prey to the most basic of problems, which is that he can't actually make anything better in terms of groceries and prices, and the people are turning on him. He's 79 in an office that accelerates aging. It doesn't help that he seems preoccupied with ballrooms and gold decor while the frustration deepens. | | ---|---|--- Kara Swisher said last week on the Pivot Tour, "I don't think Trump finishes the term." I think she may be on to something. What does this mean? Democrats will cheer that this is their chance, as in the zero-sum version of American politics, Dems feel better and better about their 2026 prospects. Most of the major Democrats are jockeying for position in the presidential primary. I spoke to South Carolina Democrats recently and they are already vetting candidates. Not much will be resolved on that side until the Democrats hash out their own leadership contest in about 28 months. That's a long time from now. The heir apparent to MAGA is J.D. Vance; he also naturally ascends as VP if Trump steps down or is indisposed for any reason. Vance and Trump's children seem to have formed a deep relationship. I'm dubious though that Vance would be able to keep MAGA together. Trump has unique appeal and his own celebrity and gravitas. He's a performer and movement leader. Those traits aren't really transferable. I think the party fragments. Marjorie Taylor Greene stepping down doesn't necessarily mean that the internal strife will end. Still, if I'm sitting here trying to figure out what comes next, so are a lot of other people. The figure who has dominated American politics for much of the last decade may be passing from view. Will Trump continue to defy gravity? This time feels different. In my experience, things often end slowly, slowly, slowly . . . and then all at once. To build the next chapter in American politics, check out - we have some very exciting announcements coming. Next up on the Tour are Boston, Baltimore, and back to NYC. Email matt@noblemobile.com if you want a few months off from your wireless bill and want to be rewarded with . My new comes out in February. Remember to look up and touch grass. | | ---|---|--- | | | | | | | (C) 2025 Andrew Yang 111 Sutter Street, 7th Floor, San Francisco, CA 94104 ? ?

The ultra MAGA extremist caucus just lost one of its loudest members: Marjorie Taylor Greene announced that she is resigning from Congress in January.
With MTG gone, now is our moment to fire another one of the absolute worst people to ever serve in the U.S. Congress: Lauren Boebert.
MTG's resignation shows that when the odds are stacked against them, the loudest MAGA voices in the room get real quiet real fast. These people are cowards, and cowards run away from a fair fight when they see that they might lose.
And now, it's Lauren Boebert's turn.

Boebert's campaign is practically broke and she is ready to throw in the towel--but it's on us to turn up the heat to show her that if she insists on running again, Trisha will beat her next November. If we work hard enough and post massive fundraising numbers, there's a good chance that Boebert will see the writing on the wall and do like MTG: resign early or retire before the election.
[So, we have to ask: would you trade $5 for kicking Lauren Boebert out of Congress? clean_body: Would you trade $5 for kicking out Lauren Boebert from Congress? With MTG gone, now is our chance to scare away another one of the absolute worst people to ever serve in the U.S. Congress. | | | | | == | Peter, The ultra MAGA extremist caucus just lost one of its loudest members: Marjorie Taylor Greene announced that she is resigning from Congress in January. With MTG gone, now is our moment to fire another one of the absolute worst people to ever serve in the U.S. Congress: Lauren Boebert. MTG's resignation shows that when the odds are stacked against them, the loudest MAGA voices in the room get real quiet real fast. These people are cowards, and cowards run away from a fair fight when they see that they might lose. And now, it's Lauren Boebert's turn. | == is only in Congress because of 546 votes. In 2024 when labor leader Trisha Calvarese and Lauren Boebert faced off, Trisha had the best performance a Democrat has ever had in this district, overperforming Kamala Harris by 6 pts whereas Boebert massively underperformed Trump in every county. Trisha outraised Boebert 5:1 in the most recent FEC report. Boebert's campaign is practically broke and she is ready to throw in the towel--but it's on us to turn up the heat to show her that if she insists on running again, Trisha will beat her next November. Thanks for reading By the Ballot! Subscribe for free to receive new posts and support my work.
[Subscribed](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYnl0aGViYWxsb3QuY29tL2FjY291bnQiLCJwIjoxNzU1NzM3MDIsInMiOjUxNTY1NjYsImYiOnRydWUsInUiOjkwNTAxMTkyLCJpYXQiOjE3NjM5OTMyNjksImV4cCI6MjA3OTU2OTI2OSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTAiLCJzdWIiOiJsaW5rLXJlZGlyZWN0In0.-9hoMPyhDOpQsP-Rg12uZXCvNJMRcwTiLsRiwfXK4ug?)
In political advertising, every cycle is a race to find the next best way to reach voters. As new platforms emerge each year, campaigns scramble to keep up -- but too often, they end up chasing trends rather than shaping them.
For nearly a decade, Facebook was the gold standard for digital persuasion. Mark Zuckerberg's platform allowed advertisers to microtarget, test, and optimize like never before. But the post-2016 reckoning -- the Cambridge Analytica scandal, congressional hearings, and a wave of privacy reforms -- changed everything. Meta rolled back political targeting capabilities, kneecapping one of the most powerful persuasion tools in modern politics.
YouTube became the next frontier, with 62% of all internet users watching daily. But as Google faced similar scrutiny, it too restricted ad targeting. Meanwhile, streaming TV surged ahead -- 82% of Americans now use streaming services, compared to just 36% with cable or satellite.
And yet, there's an even bigger opportunity we're ignoring: **digital audio**.
## **Did Video Really Kill the Radio Star?**
It may clash with where everyone thought media was heading -- and yes, cue "Video Killed the Radio Star" -- but think about it for a second. Ever since the rise of the iPhone and the explosion of wireless headphones, the most common sight in public isn't people glued to a TV or even a phone screen. It's people walking, working, commuting, or cleaning -- with headphones in.
Digital audio has quietly become the soundtrack of modern life. It makes long commutes tolerable, workouts easier, chores bearable, and everyday life just a little less dull. Multi-tasking is the norm now -- and digital audio makes it possible to stay constantly plugged into news, entertainment, and conversation without needing to look at a screen.
I myself am a voracious podcast listener for this reason. I predictably listen to _Pod Save America_ and the _Weekly Show_ because of course I do but also I listen to _Last Podcast on the Left, Behind the Bastards, the Adam Friedland Show, Stavvy 's World, Sounds like a Cult and Maintenance Phase _religiously week after week because I contain multitudes and I enjoy it. I also listen to the _Ezra Klein Show_ because I'm a masochist.
Gone are the days of rewinding tapes, burning CDs, or hoping your radio signal holds through a tunnel. Now, with a couple of taps, you can download a podcast episode, stream hours of music, or listen to an audiobook while folding laundry.
And the numbers don't lie:
> | [](https://substack.com/redirect/6ce27ffe-6fd2-4a9a-a27f-c82495a8ea57?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* **228 million Americans** (nearly 80% of everyone over 13) listen to some form of audio every month.
* **210 million** listen every single week.
* **70%** of Americans have listened to a podcast at least once.
* **55%** listen monthly -- a number that keeps growing year after year.
* In early 2025, the average American spent **3 hours and 54 minutes per day** listening to audio -- radio, podcasts, streaming music, and even satellite.

Here's what's most important: **63% of all that listening is ad-supported.**
That means while we're chasing people's eyeballs across TV, CTV, and social feeds, there's an audience of more than 200 million Americans who are literally inviting us into their ears -- and they're already used to hearing ads.
So no, video didn't kill the radio star. If anything, radio evolved, adapted, and went digital -- and now it's sitting right in the center of the attention economy, waiting for us to use it.
## **Gather Round Youths and I 'll Tell You a Tale**
But you know who loves digital audio more than any other generation? [Gen Z](https://substack.com/redirect/439c0a57-495d-4f0d-8f30-17f2a914b4fe?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Eighty-six percent of Gen Z say they listen to music or podcasts to boost their mood. They spend **42%** of their audio time streaming music, **20%** listening to YouTube, **16%** on AM/FM or radio streams, **8%** with podcasts, and **14%** with other audio sources. Basically: if it plays sound, they're tuned in.
And here's the wild part -- **they actually prefer audio-only content.**
[Seventy-six percent of Gen Z podcast listeners say ](https://substack.com/redirect/6b89908b-193f-41a7-996d-6448ff5b4ca2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)they'd rather listen than watch, even as more shows add video versions. For a generation raised on TikTok and YouTube, that's surprising -- and telling. Audio isn't background noise for them; it's a comfort zone.
Right now, about **35 million** Gen Zers are podcast listeners. And they're loyal. Once they find a podcast host or show that clicks with them, they stick with it.
As for where they're listening -- **Spotify is king.** Over half of Gen Z (56%) say it's their main app, up from 47% in 2021. YouTube trails far behind at 21%, and Apple Podcasts keeps falling -- now down to 10%.
And don't think they're doing all this in the car.
Most Gen Z listening happens **at home** and **on mobile**. They're streaming while getting ready for class, cooking dinner, or zoning out after work. Twelve percent listen on public transit, another 10% while walking or biking -- but wherever they are, they're not staring at a TV screen.
So if you're trying to reach young voters, you don't need another shiny video ad or viral stunt. You need to be where they already are -- **in their headphones, in their daily routines, and in their playlists.**
## **Whisper In My Ear, Tell Me All the Things You Want Me to Hear**
Here's the thing: **audio ads work** -- and they work better than almost anything else in your toolkit.
[One study from Veritonic](https://substack.com/redirect/60d364b9-95d1-4203-966e-618b4424a3f3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) found that **60% of consumers remember audio and podcast ads more than billboards.** Another showed that nearly **half of all listeners recall audio ads more than display or video ads.**
And it's not just recall -- it's attention.
[A Dentsu study](https://substack.com/redirect/60d364b9-95d1-4203-966e-618b4424a3f3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) revealed that people pay **128% more attention to audio ads than to TV commercials.** Think about that for a second: in an age of constant distraction, people are actually _listening_.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/ab20c67d-b18b-4317-9e7d-d338aebc36a8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
When you're literally in someone's ear -- on their walk, in their car, while they're cooking dinner -- your message doesn't compete with a million visuals on a feed. It's direct, personal, and intimate. You're not interrupting their routine -- you're part of it.
And it's cost-effective.
Podcast ads are **43% more cost-efficient per CPM** than other platforms, according to Dentsu. That means you can get better recall, more attention, and deeper engagement -- for less money.
That's an equation that any campaign manager or media buyer should love.
Audio also has another advantage most don't think about: **trust.
** Listeners build a bond with podcast hosts -- it's the modern version of appointment radio. When those hosts read or endorse an ad, the message lands differently. It doesn't feel transactional; it feels conversational. That's why small brands (and political outsiders) have thrived on audio -- the connection is real.
So while we're spending billions trying to chase eyeballs, there's an entire ecosystem of ears waiting -- focused, loyal, and ready to engage.
## **Ear Piercing (Through the Clutter)**
Let's face it -- we are drowning in ads. Everywhere you scroll, tap, or swipe, there's another video trying to sell you something, another campaign "reaching out," another sponsored post wedged between your playlists. In politics, it's even worse.
The 2024 cycle was the **most expensive in history** -- with Democrats spending **$6.7 billion** , Republicans **$7.6 billion** , and another **$500 million** from third-party candidates. That's more than $14 billion collectively, and what did most of that money go toward? TV spots, social ads, and every other place people are already sick of being sold to.
We're competing for attention in the noisiest marketplace in human history. And persuasion now takes more than a clever tagline or a viral video. People are tuning out -- or actively muting us.
That's why **audio** is such an opportunity.
It's not just another channel; it's an escape hatch. While everyone else is screaming visually, audio lets us whisper directly to voters who _actually_ want to listen.
Think about it: when you're on a run, driving home, cleaning the kitchen, or commuting -- that's the one time you're not doom-scrolling. You're focused. You're open. You're listening. That's where persuasion still works.
By investing more in audio advertising -- podcasts, streaming radio, in-app placements, and even the new wave of **AI-narrated articles** that are popping up across major outlets -- Democrats can reach audiences who have completely tuned out of traditional visual ads. More and more publishers are monetizing their text-to-speech features with **15- and 30-second ad slots** at the start or midpoint of each piece. That's inventory we're barely even touching.
And this shouldn't stop at paid placements. Campaigns should be thinking **organically** , too. That means candidates guesting on popular podcasts, campaign managers doing longform interviews, and yes -- even starting their own shows. People want authenticity, and audio gives it room to breathe.
You're already seeing this shift. Former operatives like **Ezra Klein** have built entire media ecosystems around their voices. Governors like **Andy Beshear** are launching their own podcasts to speak directly to voters -- unfiltered, without a journalist acting as a middleman. That's the model.
So it's time to stop thinking about what's "eye-catching" and start thinking about what's **ear-grabbing**.
## **TL;DR**
Campaigns are still fighting for attention where voters have long stopped listening. While billions pour into TV, video, and social media ads, the real untapped frontier in political communication is **digital audio**.
Nearly **80% of Americans** listen to some form of digital audio weekly, and **Gen Z leads the charge** -- spending hours a day streaming music and podcasts. Audio ads don't just reach them; they _stick_. Studies show they're **128% more engaging than TV ads** , drive better recall, and cost less per impression.
For Democrats especially, this is the next great messaging opportunity. In an oversaturated visual landscape, **audio cuts through the noise** -- literally. It's intimate, portable, and trusted. Whether through ads on Spotify, podcast sponsorships, or candidates hosting their own shows, the campaigns that speak directly into voters' headphones will shape the next generation of persuasion.
Bottom line: stop asking what's eye-catching -- start thinking about what's **ear-grabbing**.

Non-Contribution Account (For TTPAC, Inc.’s Expenditures Other Than Direct Contributions to Federal Committees)

As permitted by law, TTPAC, Inc. maintains a non-contribution account for the purpose of influencing federal, state, and local elections. Federal law requires us to use our best efforts to collect and report the name, address, occupation, and employer of individuals whose contribution exceeds $200 in an election cycle. For contributions from federal committees, corporations, and labor unions - regardless of contribution amount - federal law requires us to use our best efforts to collect and report the committee’s name and address. State and local law may require us to collect and report additional information.

Contributions to this account may be used to influence federal elections by making independent expenditures in support of or in opposition to federal candidates. Additionally, in state and local jurisdictions that permit non-individual type (e.g., corporate) contributions directly to candidates and/or committees, contributions to this account may be used for both independent expenditures and direct contributions to candidates and/or committees, as permitted by state and local law.

Individuals, federal committees, corporations, and labor unions may contribute to TTPAC, Inc.’s non-contribution account without limit. Contributions by national banks, foreign nationals (without lawful permanent resident status), and other foreign entities (e.g., foreign corporations) are prohibited. Contributions to TTPAC, Inc.’s non-contribution account are not tax-deductible for federal income tax purposes.

name: Newt Gingrich
subject: Another AOC, Ilhan Omar, Rashida Tlaib & Jasmine Crockett
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Matt Van Epps for Congress
body:
| | | | Peter,
If you agree that we don’t need another AOC, Ilhan Omar, Rashida Tlaib, or Jasmine Crockett running around Congress, keep reading.

When I was Speaker of the House, I learned firsthand how **every single race matters**. Given how narrow the GOP majority is now, just _**one vote**_ could decide whether we pass more of President Trump’s America First agenda or see it stall out.

**That’s why I’m writing to you today about a critical special election on December 2nd in Tennessee.**

**Matt Van Epps, a West Point graduate, combat veteran, and America First Patriot who has been endorsed by President Trump himself is going up against a radical Leftist they are already calling “the AOC of Tennessee."**
---
|
---
[PROTECT THE MAJORITY](https://links.iterable.com/s/c/tw901-vTEblL_YAlQYSKORxnBBxtLsLdOuG6ABH7sYwhg-n_toCfZ53YdMC5Lx_7pz6-sMOsjMEezyv-nIjIgl_A9xRgyQZhvUeEOyUJaQz7_UbYObl15X5wRBRMe7WRD-4QdK7R-eIm8IPbjc_yd48eQJiK7-me4jLD4qxQWeF7fGRHmYwynaqxxllQVqWcqarLk3dFLMKhS-W405fzdVRTKVmDlJpXV29O6U3kopffq_3ktmcEnx-6D6Z3BzaZTLhjiqEo60hJhXbL0IP4V9NCVLSwK2YWJvjIBQpGF976bGmo6l2hGhJmvtpSssM-UtKY5QoCyDsKni_vFXSIGuTeEuF8Gpv14UC8a7KQdqjx6JYs0Ry7DwBPgat15D9meKrw2ESJ36QfZg9VWyUdY01bpP2Md_jEOX8bij_3LCkfoxizCJISqhUnaA9OMV69T_ePWMd6XV_9v7oFHSlFVz8UkoKsY-qOC3PNcizKc-ng6SQzOnHXQkk0rPHsqmcpYgGo8Moun8ZpytFQmNDXb1FjxGsYcfSJuzBj36nmtncJeSfaXlCIMG2jkyKm7Z9IME0XmP2erjcmrzxOsADEIAE_TSXHsLyS9nOfAtNOY9a4HAnX3KQfPqumynLGzywMiHQ9gkwVfciYWGAxXzf_X1rQyo1pYfNNjw6SXGy95RdMlHkAzfw1V6QK-BjkrmoiYJbk3zowpWM-mAQ5BeGke_jvgA/7NJkJUJxgIsNq2rxekMtA5HIi9VFNUaR/15)
|
---
If we lose this race, it won’t just be a symbolic defeat – _**it could destroy our House majority.**_

The Democrat running against him is so radical that she filmed herself mocking and bullying ICE agents during an immigration raid. She’d be just as bad as the rest of the “Squad” in Congress.

**That’s how high the stakes are.**

**Winning this race means strengthening our House majority, advancing President Trump’s policies, and building momentum for the midterms. Losing it means empowering the Left to block everything we’ve worked for.**

_We can’t let that happen._
**
[I’m asking you – as someone who knows what it takes to win tough races – to make a contribution right now to help push Matt Van Epps across the finish line.](https://links.iterable.com/s/c/FzVpNkiUacceE0XEZJL7hRwtpVxeFEIErGiMW_ykRdHw7G4KeJfs4C0sxaJaP084fiB8cG1Mw3bxKubK-KXDfz16EVMxN_Wb42O90nn-aowEhk97iLRBQEKiov60uWEMwDDsuJAeuTYbiU-I2hDp7Tn6Z2RUxz5f0AAsBN12MldUfeEKZbxHxj6dBpUscd4Tnlo1c5GsGifclkxab4mzOYztcVCQe3HFJhTFlcA9TlXKYKULtGxOFXouKT5Bpb8gMDq1Q9tkxU--Am1ArM81lERBnFYFSvucDLiNIGL6A9_ZEHUbBOPhJVOat0TVsDp2YsRTehC-rG6rMgtwL8qjcFRrqKSdLWvqWPJ_Rd3-AwBN0GJnbVGpFF6wQHGyNIKV4DFbOkiZveLrdy68lQJ1qNUKyBOJRKrOEq4ofI_8Y8qrnFY3HokCi9jDJlfzdQISWtV8tWhDu0884nvCBNR6Lk4GRM-WcYZXBwNMx44FJVWXNgwe6sURLDyQVRiGEiIHW9EJCG05v1_NodRufCcJP70REYvHVPie80gG7Vx77yqKcp2kv7Np6PSynf9lQGe-AamBJmWTcfLKxoBngKgLGz_tb3__DUUisF2ELYjnfQrmJpYOkKad9_W1oJ0ggdLbgIj01b2Mz0S0kAJPA4BbxqCFiG_t5PBIaem9wzCzCdSErfqKqpU4eMbY1v0AqW74aukeJE7_ZQZcf7eLXRoJsr6nWA/UiiVtpMhAnhztrecN9H2T07V3BnzoQfQ/15)**
This race is a test for our movement. Let’s prove once again that the American people stand with President Trump and the America First agenda.

Thank you for your leadership and support,

Your friend,
Newt Gingrich

name: NRSC HQ
subject: FINAL NOTICE: Your record could be erased tonight
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the NRSC
body:

Non-Contribution Account (For TTPAC, Inc.’s Expenditures Other Than Direct Contributions to Federal Committees)

As permitted by law, TTPAC, Inc. maintains a non-contribution account for the purpose of influencing federal, state, and local elections. For contributions from federal committees, corporations, and labor unions - regardless of contribution amount - federal law requires us to use our best efforts to collect and report the committee’s name and address. Contributions to this account may be used to influence federal elections by making independent expenditures in support of or in opposition to federal candidates. Additionally, in state and local jurisdictions that permit non-individual type (e.g., corporate) contributions directly to candidates and/or committees, contributions to this account may be used for both independent expenditures and direct contributions to candidates and/or committees, as permitted by state and local law.

Individuals, federal committees, corporations, and labor unions may contribute to TTPAC, Inc.’s non-contribution account without limit. Contributions by national banks, foreign nationals (without lawful permanent resident status), and other foreign entities (e.g., foreign corporations) are prohibited. A self-declared socialist, Mamdani wants to “globalize the intifada” and import values into the West that will destroy New York City and destabilize our civilization. Everything about him runs counter to America First. The fact that he WON in arguably America's greatest city proves that, while we won Congress and the White House,the fight for the heart, soul, and future of America still rages on. Turning Point PAC is working harder than ever to . We’re carrying on Charlie’s fight to ensure that radical socialists like Mamdani do NOT gain power in other cities, counties, and states across the country. For America, Turning Point PAC Non-Contribution Account (For TTPAC, Inc.’s Expenditures Other Than Direct Contributions to Federal Committees) As permitted by law, TTPAC, Inc. maintains a non-contribution account for the purpose of influencing federal, state, and local elections. Federal law requires us to use our best efforts to collect and report the name, address, occupation, and employer of individuals whose contribution exceeds $200 in an election cycle. For contributions from federal committees, corporations, and labor unions - regardless of contribution amount - federal law requires us to use our best efforts to collect and report the committee’s name and address. State and local law may require us to collect and report additional information. Contributions to this account may be used to influence federal elections by making independent expenditures in support of or in opposition to federal candidates. Additionally, in state and local jurisdictions that permit non-individual type (e.g., corporate) contributions directly to candidates and/or committees, contributions to this account may be used for both independent expenditures and direct contributions to candidates and/or committees, as permitted by state and local law. Individuals, federal committees, corporations, and labor unions may contribute to TTPAC, Inc.’s non-contribution account without limit. Contributions by national banks, foreign nationals (without lawful permanent resident status), and other foreign entities (e.g., foreign corporations) are prohibited. Contributions to TTPAC, Inc.’s non-contribution account are not tax-deductible for federal income tax purposes. Paid for by Turning Point PAC, Inc. name: =?UTF-8?Q?James_O=E2=80=99Keefe?=
subject: The insiders are wired and ready — but this story dies without $38k in 10 days
body:
I still get chills thinking about what we pulled off together last month.
[View in Web Browser](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.com/click/e10211932/VaHR0cHM6Ly93cm8ubnVjbGV1c2VtYWlsLmNvbS9hbXBsaWZ5L3YvQWZNcWZHZXJENw/SZW1haWxfYW1lcmljYU9ORXN0d2Fycm9vbV9jb20yNTExMjQ/LbmNsRFpRU3JibQ/qP2hpZHM9bmNuekJTSzFadXhieWVscw/gaSRqJA/s9m23d250dd)

| | | | [****](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)
[****](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.) Dear Patriot,

I still get chills thinking about what we pulled off together last month.

Because we kept our cameras rolling, we exposed that massive 8(a) contracting scam — executives on hidden tape admitting they were gaming the system for hundreds of millions. Tribal members we interviewed called it straight: "They're renting our sovereignty."

And it worked. The SBA suspended ATI Government Solutions. The Treasury Department launched a **$9 billion investigation**.

Real fraudsters are sweating, real money is being clawed back for taxpayers — **all because a few thousand everyday Americans like you said, "Enough is enough.**

[**And I need your help to keep the pressure on in every direction.**](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)

That's the power we have when we stand together. No corporate overlords. No government strings. Just pure, citizen-funded journalism that forces accountability. [**Are you in**](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)?

But right now, I'm writing you from the war room because we've got something even larger locked in — insiders ready to blow open the kind of entrenched corruption that makes the 8(a) mess look like small change.

The kind involving leverage, compromise, the shadows where power really hides.

The team is geared up and raring to go. Whistleblowers are putting everything on the line.

[**But we're staring at a**](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)**[**$38,000 shortfall**](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)**[**to launch safely**](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.): cross-country flights for undercover meets, secure tech that can't be traced, legal shields for sources, and keeping paychecks coming for the staff who work 80-hour weeks without complaint because they believe in this mission as much as I do.

We've got **10 days** before this window slams shut. If we miss it, the evidence gets destroyed, sources get scared off, and the story dies. [**Chip in to help cover these costs so we can bring the truth to the light.**](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)

When I started my career as an investigative journalist, I was scraping by on credit cards and prayers. Then the FBI raided me, the lawsuits piled up — and it was only because Americans like you stood by me. [**I need you again now**](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.).

I'm a journalist who's paid a price for this work — arrests, betrayals, everything but regret.

And the one thing that keeps me going is knowing there are friends like you who understand: **if the price of truth isn't your life, then you're for sale**.

This is that moment again. [**Four hundred of us at $100 closes the gap tonight**](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.). A few stepping up bigger gets the cameras rolling by morning.

[**Will you stand with me one more time?**](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.) Whatever you can give today fuels the exposé that could change everything.

[**Rush your gift here.**](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)

From the bottom of my heart — thank you for being the reason we're still in this fight.

In Truth,
James O’Keefe
Founder and CEO
O'Keefe Media Group

P.S. When this drops and the headlines explode (and they will), I want you to remember: [**you were the one who made it possible. Sunlight is still the best disinfectant — and you're holding the torch.**](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)

---
[****](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)
---

This email was sent to **[dpwillis67@gmail.com](mailto:dpwillis67@gmail.com)** as part of your subscription to **America First War Room**

108 S Washington St. Suite 200
Alexandria, VA 22314

[Unsubscribe](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.com/click/e10211932/VaHR0cHM6Ly93cm8ubnVjbGV1c2VtYWlsLmNvbS9hbXBsaWZ5L3N1YnNjcmlwdGlvbnMvdW5zdWJzY3JpYmU/SZW1haWxfYW1lcmljYU9ORXN0d2Fycm9vbV9jb20yNTExMjQ/LbmNsRFpRU3JibQ/qP2hpZHM9bmNuekJTSzFadXhieWVscw/gaSRqJA/s3g4a7d693d)

subject: Our culture has a sick hatred for integrity and achievement
body:

Let me tell you more about The Atlas Society and how they are fundamentally changing the direction of our country.

_I will also tell you HOW The Atlas Society is spreading its message in what I consider one of the most innovative methods I 've EVER seen a nonprofit organization engage in before._

_**_So keep reading_!**_

My mentor, Andrew Breitbart, reminds us that politics is downstream from culture. But what is culture downstream from? Answer: Philosophy. That's where The Atlas Society shines, engaging young people with the virtues of reason, achievement, and benevolent self-interest in wildly creative, artistic ways.

Have you ever read Ayn Rand's _Atlas Shrugged_ or _The Fountainhead_?

These books changed how I saw the world and my role in it.

But they are 700+ pages long!

**Jennifer shared this terrifying statistic with me:** Daily reading of books among young people has fallen from 70% in the late 1970s to 12% today.

So, how do we preserve Rand's role as the greatest all-time recruiter for liberty? That's where The Atlas Society gets creative! They leverage art and technology to create programming for "_skimmers, swimmers, and deep divers_."

---
**Skimmers:** Social media memes and animated videos -- every single one of their 50 posts a week gets 40K comments, shares, and likes. The "Draw My Life" videos on concepts like Capitalism, Property Rights, and Free Speech reach MILLIONS of viewers. Links invite young people on a journey for deeper intellectual exploration.

**Swimmers:** Graphic novel adaptations of Ayn Rand's shorter books, Pocket Guides that distill big concepts like Postmodernism and Socialism into a 90-minute read. Weekly podcasts with top entrepreneurs and thought-leaders, and discussion groups like "Morals & Markets."

**Deep Divers:** They recruited an all-star academic faculty of philosophers and economists who guide students on a deeper study of Rand's philosophy, publish independent work, and provide programming at their summer conference for students (the Galt's Gulch Summit, where I spoke!).

clean_body: The media launched a vicious smear campaign against me The Atlas Society proudly stood by my side. | | Peter, I learned firsthand how our culture despises achievement and wishes to destroy anyone willing to challenge the status quo, big government, or the Fake News media machine. When I was ousted from Project Veritas, two big things (one you haven't heard about) happened to me. FIRST: The media launched a vicious smear campaign against me like I've never seen before. Make no mistake: They identified this as an opportunity to "cancel" me forever and tried everything in their power to destroy me and everything I've ever stood for. They failed. SECOND: I received an OVERWHELMING amount of support from patriots across the country. These brave men and women defended me, but more importantly, they stood up for the principles of liberty and free speech. One of my top supporters was The Atlas Society. Jennifer Grossman, the CEO of The Atlas Society, proudly stood by my side and even invited me to speak with a gathering of the organization's top student leaders about free speech and how Howard Roark, the fictional hero of The Fountainhead by Ayn Rand, remains my inspiration to act with integrity. Which is why I am personally reaching out to you. I told Jennifer I would help The Atlas Society raise $25,000 in the next 48 hours by personally contacting my online supporters. Let me tell you more about The Atlas Society and how they are fundamentally changing the direction of our country. I will also tell you HOW The Atlas Society is spreading its message in what I consider one of the most innovative methods I 've EVER seen a nonprofit organization engage in before. So keep reading! My mentor, Andrew Breitbart, reminds us that politics is downstream from culture. But what is culture downstream from? Answer: Philosophy. That's where The Atlas Society shines, engaging young people with the virtues of reason, achievement, and benevolent self-interest in wildly creative, artistic ways. Have you ever read Ayn Rand's Atlas Shrugged or The Fountainhead? These books changed how I saw the world and my role in it. But they are 700+ pages long! Jennifer shared this terrifying statistic with me: Daily reading of books among young people has fallen from 70% in the late 1970s to 12% today. So, how do we preserve Rand's role as the greatest all-time recruiter for liberty? That's where The Atlas Society gets creative! They leverage art and technology to create programming for "skimmers, swimmers, and deep divers." Skimmers: Social media memes and animated videos -- every single one of their 50 posts a week gets 40K comments, shares, and likes. The "Draw My Life" videos on concepts like Capitalism, Property Rights, and Free Speech reach MILLIONS of viewers. Links invite young people on a journey for deeper intellectual exploration. Swimmers: Graphic novel adaptations of Ayn Rand's shorter books, Pocket Guides that distill big concepts like Postmodernism and Socialism into a 90-minute read. Weekly podcasts with top entrepreneurs and thought-leaders, and discussion groups like "Morals & Markets." Deep Divers: They recruited an all-star academic faculty of philosophers and economists who guide students on a deeper study of Rand's philosophy, publish independent work, and provide programming at their summer conference for students (the Galt's Gulch Summit, where I spoke!). Jennifer and The Atlas Society are accomplishing great things for our country, and I hope you will be one of the FIRST supporters of mine to make a donation and help me reach our $25,000 goal. Please use one of these links to donate: Ultimately, here is what The Atlas Society is fighting FOR: Free market capitalism Rational self-interest Respect for achievement and success Free speech The future of young people and our great country And here is what The Atlas Society is fighting AGAINST: Cancel Culture Our Educational system indoctrinating young people to embrace socialism Our sick culture that demonizes success and embraces twisted ideas * Big government I am inviting you to support The Atlas Society with a donation of ANY amount. As a personal favor to me, please make your donation within the next 48 hours so I can help Jennifer reach our goal of $25,000. Thank you for everything, James O'Keefe P.S. Here is a picture of me speaking with The Atlas Society's top student leaders. These students inspire me. Sadly, young people are learning to embrace socialism, reject critical thinking skills, and shed their creativity. Well, NOT these students. The Atlas Society is teaching students that they CAN accomplish great things, become leaders, and inspire others to do the same. Thank you, James The Atlas Society is a 501(c)(3) Not For Profit Organization. All donations are tax-deductible. 1038 Miwok Dr Lodi, CA 95240

name: Niki Conforti
subject: Are you surprised?
body:
Are you surprised? | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/df5bf952cd38/conforti-for-congress-10611993?e=25f4fa3311)
---
| | |
---
| | | Dear Peter, Once again, Sean Casten takes a stand against the very people he represents. He has put politics over people - and voted AGAINST opening the government!

His vote is a clear sign he does not care about his constituents. He would rather see families go bankrupt and children starve than vote to end the shutdown.

The recent 'Schumer Shutdown' was the longest on record - 43 days. Casten and his fellow Democrats pushed to keep the government closed in efforts to fund healthcare for illegals. In the end, 8 Dem senators, including Dick Durbin, crossed the aisle to open the government. When the vote went back to the House, Casten voted to keep it closed!

Casten is a parrot for the far-Left agenda. He has no interest in doing what is right for people of the 6th District. He only cares about his own power. Having spent many years delivering healthcare, I plan to use my experience to benefit the people of the 6th District, and all Americans with some level of reform in the HC system.

Help me make a difference and bring common sense back to WDC! Please consider a contribution to help me take back our District. You can be a part of the movement to turn District 6 RED again!

Click the link below to make a donation today.

[](https://conforti4congress.us6.list-manage.com/track/click?u=7fd34b7284bf7f5e87a4e45e5&id=54a4d8bc80&e=25f4fa3311)

Don't stop believing in what we can achieve when we work together!
Your support is greatly appreciated.
---
| Thank you,

Niki Conforti
Candidate
Illinois 6th Congressional District
---

| | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---

The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Four strategic air bases. Dozens of damaged airplanes. Drones went through all the air defenses.

This is not a raid. This is a new era of warfare.
